behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in module handoff/consistency between stored per-dimension findings and summary persistence for the same round. Behavior text is about stored state interaction, not a single pure function. No real OS/network/subprocess or model-output dependence is stated, so Integration is the lowest level.
behaviors.test_description: Write per-dimension findings for round R including at least one FAIL, then attempt to persist a PASS summary for round R through the normal service/repository path; assert the write is rejected and no PASS summary is stored.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: This specifically targets wiring between gate computation and findings storage, with an intentionally misleading summary text. The needed signal is cross-module read-path correctness. No requirement for real infra or model judgment appears in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored findings for a round with a FAIL, set summary text to manual 'all PASS', run stop-gate computation via production wiring, and assert verdict follows stored findings (not summary text).

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is policy + persistence: flag-gated override plus warning record creation. It requires module interaction (request handling, policy check, audit storage). The text does not require real network/OS/subprocess, and does not depend on AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Attempt manual override without override flag and assert rejection. Repeat with explicit override flag and assert success plus persisted warning/audit record retrievable from the audit store.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: This behavior hinges on real commit/update chronology ('after the latest fix commit') and enforcing PASS finalization against that boundary. Verifying this against actual VCS/subprocess state provides reality signal that in-process fakes can miss. Rejected lower level because the phrase 'latest fix commit' implies real infra-backed commit state.
behaviors.test_description: Create commit C1, run review round R1 linked to C1, then create fix commit C2. Try to finalize PASS with R1 and assert block. Run a new round R2 after C2 and assert PASS can proceed only with R2.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on a 'live review-agent round' on updated diff. Even if assertion is deterministic (fresh-round required), the execution path includes a real model call, and mocks can hide failures tied to real agent execution/registration of a fresh run. Rejected Workflow because the behavior needs one fresh agentic decision gate, not necessarily multi-agent chained decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In a live run with a real review agent, update the diff and attempt PASS without rerun (must fail). Then run a fresh review-agent round on the updated diff and assert PASS finalization is only eligible after that fresh round is recorded.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic policy over stored findings + round-budget state. The failure mode is cross-module state evaluation (results store + gate policy), not real infra or model-output quality. Behavior text references 'stored results', which disqualifies needing agentic execution here.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored results with unresolved MUST-FIX findings and set rounds-used == rounds-allowed; run outcome computation and assert escalation is emitted and PASS is blocked.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly says 'live multi-round review workflow', requiring sequenced rounds and terminal-state handling across rounds. That is beyond single-step agentic validation and must be checked end-to-end across the pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a live multi-round review pipeline: run successive review rounds, keep unresolved MUST-FIX findings, exhaust allowed rounds, then assert PASS remains blocked and escalation is emitted in final workflow state/output.
